reviews.review_msg: DO NOT TAKE, doesn't really teach you anything, have to learn everything on your own. No practice during the class, if it delays your graduation let it, not worth taking the course with her.

DO NOT TAKE DO NOT TAKE DO NOT TAKE. I have never written a review or even look at this site but I have to for this. She is by FAR the worst prof in existence. Ive never been so worried about failing a class. These aren't hard concepts she it just brutal at teaching and testing. She makes a mid difficult class a TRAMA-INDUCED NIGHTMARE. NO!
Slides are awful, unclear what is being taught. RUN RUN RUN

Her teaching skills are completely inadequate. Laying the PPT out on the table and just reading it without any interaction with the students. If you unfortunately take her course, you can only pray for the best.
She made the already difficult course even worse. Don't choose her section at all.

Among all the courses in Finance, this one is the most difficult. She simply had no idea what she was doing in class. The students kept reading the PPT and didn't respond. So I decided to skip a chapter and keep adding more content, not following the prescribed chapters as stated in the syllabus. Terrible!
She doesn't know how to teach, 1 session have 3-4 chapters, good luck
Don't take her course, run!!!!!
She is so terrible, I never thought a course have that much chapters to learn. Avoid

Just did her exam. Guy sitting behind me lost 2 years of his life. The TA Terrell didn't help anyone when they had a question except on girl. What's the point of him even being there if he can't clarify questions.
Just finished her final. I think I lost around 2 years of my life expectancy.

By FAR the worst professor I've ever had. Mumbles in a small class of 40 students, and when you ask her to speak up she doesn't. Doesn't teach anything, puts up slides (which make 0 sense) and reads it WORD FOR WORD. Quizzes are 30 questions in 30 mins. Actually so mind blown by HOW BADDDDD SHE IS I AM IN SHOCK.

Please do yourself a favour by not taking her class. As excited as I was when I took the class, I've lost the excitement, motivation, and even the courage to take her exam. I'm thinking about switching concentrations, my mind can't think straight about finance, the same concentration I was looking forward to studying for years. HORRIBLE!
IDK is this course hard, or you make it that hard. Greta, please bless us and retire. No offense

She does not care about her students, she does the bare minimum and if anyone doesn't understand a concept she blames it on them. It is nearly impossible to follow what she is saying during lectures as she speaks extremely quickly and quietly. At times it almost seems like she doesn't even know what she is saying. Stay away.

For a professor to be teaching a course for this many years with no improvement in course delivery, SFU needs to fire her. It is unfair that students continue to suffer from her lectures consisting of reading slides and whispering, along with formulas with no explanation in application. Avoid her at all costs.
Greta if your reading this. Please bless the students and retire.

The first 1-star review was on August 18, 2010. You would think that a professor would make adjustments. 15 years later, students are still in the same situation... SFU, please sit in on one of her lectures, and I guarantee that you will leave a bad review yourself.

I really wish Sfu reads these comments. Greta doesn't teach, she reads off the slides. If a student asks her a question, she doesn't answer the question but goes off topic with another answer. Even if she is your only option, DO NOT TAKE HER!. Even if it will delay your graduation, DO NOT TAKE HER!
I can't describe the number of times I wished I had a different professor for this class. The ratings speak for themself. 30 1/5 ratings. That 5/5 rating is not true, don't listen to it.

Worst teacher ive ever had at SFU. Whispers in class, and when someone called her out she told them to sit closer. Slides are horrible, as formulas are just given with no legend explaining what variables mean, or even what the formula is used for. SFU needs to re-evaluate her as a teacher here.
Currently studying for her final right now, and it made me want to write another review. DON'T TAKE HER. I wish I had taken this class in another semester.

Completely self-learning class. She's basically whispering during her lecture which makes it near impossible to understand what she's saying. The classroom is basically a tutorial sized classroom. And she thought it was a good idea to have an assignment due on the 9th when people have finals to study for.
terrible. straight up terrible. might have to switch concentrations cause of her.
Is unable to teach the concepts clearly. Self learning class.
Worst Lecturer I've ever had. Avoid at all cost.
This class single handedly destroyed my will to live
Would NOT recommend.

Hardly complain about profs, but Greta is brutal. Very difficult to understand, not because of an accent but a horrendous ability to explain concepts. Spends years on pointless terms then whips through the mathematical portions. It's a self-learning class. Her TA Terrell is an inaccurate marker.  Pros- Straightforward exams, tries(ish) to help.
Greta is a nice person, but her lectures are sometimes hard to understand because she speaks fast. However, the averages are low so it is not hard to beat them.

I'll start by saying that the midterm and final averages were in the 40s. This is with a 10% completion mark for assignments, increasing the weight of those exams. She can't teach and hates that students don't attend her lectures in a never-ending cycle. She is a nice person, but a terrible professor, not sure how she is tenured.

She is outrightly the worst professor I have encountered until now at Beedie. She is not fit to be an educator at any scholastic level. Further, she mumbles and speaks at an astronomical pace which no one can really comprehend. Narrates off slides, cancels classes, scrambles material and sets terribly hard examinations (averages were insanely low).
she should take EDUC 100 instead of teaching... (she'd probably fail)

Assignments are too simple to cover all materials of the chapter. She rushed through everything and did not even take effort to explain the material properly with examples. 
Midterms were fair if you did all the end of chapter exercises in the textbook, but final is terrible. She even canceled a class without any explainations.

I have had two classes with Greta and I would highly recommend avoiding her. She showed up late to every lecture and often rushes through content. The assignments were fairly basic and seem easy but then the exams were exponentially more difficult. Every exam I have done with her had a class avg of under 45%. She is nice but not a helpful prof.
If you can avoid, do yourself a favour.

She is very unorganized. She canceled classes and scrambled everything in the end. Her midterms will be a lot harder than the assignments and will require to memorize all the terms. Highly recommend getting the textbook and do the practice problems as her assignment and midterms will be from the textbook. If you can, just avoid her.
The worst professor I have ever had by far, not even close. Do not take any classes with her. AVOID at ALL COSTS.
Really Bad Professor. Inaccessible and does not answer emails or questions.

Easily the worst Professor I have had in my 4 years here at SFU. Makes concepts much more complex then they need to be. You can barely understand what she says because of her accent. She will never respond to your emails even though she says to email her if anyone has any questions. 

Do yourself a favor and save your sanity by not taking her.

She is an outstanding finance lecturer whose dedication and expertise significantly contributed to students' understanding of the subject. With an engaging teaching style, she adeptly demystified complex financial concepts, making them accessible and relatable. She went above and beyond to support her students' learning journey.

THE WORST professor I had in my whole 4 year degree. She has ruined my interest in finance. I got the class average grade by navigating everything through YouTube. DO NOT take her. You will just cry and learn nothing...I am serious NOTHING! I am planning to retake this course just to better understand the concepts. (Inspire I got a B).

Worst professor I have ever had at Beedie. I was thinking about concentrating in finance but she has ruined that for me in one semester. The first midterm average was 35% and second midterm average was 50%. If you're considering her DO NOT take it. If possible, just wait for another semester and get a better prof because you will not learn and cry
She isn't bad, I mean she is nice, she tries to help. The chapters we tried problem questions from were ok, but for me I can't get anything out of slides.
Slides are awful, unclear what is being taught. Tests unrelated to course material

She mumbles a lot, her writing is a bit hard to read, and she goes really fast through her lectures. Sometimes it's hard to grasp concepts as she goes too fast and her exams require you to know everything to do well. Exam averages are low as people are quite lost in the class sometimes.

Nice-ish lady but belittles dumb questions, skims everything difficult. Extremely unorganized, slide deck useless, hw assignments r way easier than exams. Class is mandatoryish to ask questions, not for lecture content. Accent really isn't bad, but a huge mumbler and time waster. Be very direct when you have questions/ choose a different prof.
She is a nice person and cares about students. However, she is extremely unorganized and has a lot of mumbling. She can't even solve the problem from her midterm. I would not recommend her.
Very confusing professor

Greta may speak too fast at times, and she does have somewhat accent, but its not too bad. Her class is easier than blazenkos cuz her averages are lower. for the quizzes and the midterm, just do the past ones and ull be fine. for the final, make sure you're paying attention throughout the classes, as some theory questions she discuses in class come

Greta is the absolute worst professor I've ever had. She recycles exams that George Blazenko used in the past for us to use as study guides. However, her exam ends up being different and not similar at all. Useless.
helpless, never assign any questions but ask theoretical ques on quiz and exam never mentioned before.

The class was very very confusing. She speaks way too fast and mumbles and asks the class if they understand, then doesn't think to reteach the concepts when everyone is obviously completely lost. The final was far harder than the assignments and you needed deeper knowledge of the material than she taught. Would not recommend this class to anyone.

Greta's class is brutal. Her accent isn't too bad, but she talks WAY too fast and mumbles and expects people to just get it. She's constantly making mistakes in her own work that students have to mention to her to fix. She doesn't teach you how the process of figuring out the question works, just gives you a bunch of equations. Final was very hard

Quiz is easy. Its the exact same questions from the solution manual so you can easily just memorize it. Midterm was hard avg was 55%. Final I thought was fair until I saw my mark. She teaches fast and the examples she gives are easier than the questions she posts on the exams. I wouldn't take her again.

She is the worth prof I ever Had in SFU. Horrible. The point is the class ever teaches you anything, I learned everything by the text book. She just reads off slides ( you are not even her own slides). At the end you probably will get average grade, but you paid your tuition to learn nothing. Thought BUS 316 suppose to be interesting too =(
